title: Indie SaaS Monthly
subject: Indie SaaS Monthly — March 2026
summary: Solo founder sold AI-built product Base44 to Wix for $80M; built with no team and no funding
Y Combinator reported average time-to-MVP decreased 60% vs 2022 for AI-native startups
Spec-first development (writing requirements before letting agents code) emerging as the dominant build pattern

stories.headline: How Solo Founders Are Shipping MVPs in Weeks
bullets: Standard AI-first flow: idea validation via LLM research, spec writing, agent-driven development, automated testing, deploy to Vercel
Founders using Claude Code + Cursor combo: Cursor for daily edits, Claude Code for architecture and multi-file scaffolding
Bolt.new and Lovable gaining traction for generating full-stack prototypes from prompts in under an hour

stories.headline: Spec-First Development: The Emerging Pattern
bullets: Top builders write detailed requirements and architecture docs (CLAUDE.md, specs) before touching code
Amazon's Kiro IDE formalizes this: generates requirements, design specs, and task breakdowns as a first step
Pattern works because agents perform dramatically better with clear constraints and context

stories.headline: Base44: $0 to $80M Acquisition, Built Solo with AI
bullets: Maor Shlomo built Base44 (no-code platform) entirely solo using AI coding tools
Reached 250K users before Wix acquired it for $80M cash
No funding, no team, no traditional marketing; product-led growth only

stories.headline: HeadshotPro: $300K/Month as a One-Person Operation
bullets: Danny Postma running HeadshotPro solo at $300K MRR using AI for product development and operations
Sarah Chen launched AI design agency, hit $420K ARR within 8 months
Operating margins of 60-80% vs 10-20% for traditionally staffed SaaS businesses

stories.headline: The Sub-$50/Month SaaS Factory Stack
bullets: Next.js + Tailwind on Vercel, Supabase or Neon for DB, Clerk or NextAuth for auth, Stripe for payments
Full solopreneur stack runs $3K-$12K/year (95-98% cost reduction vs hiring)
Boilerplate starters (ShipFast, Supastarter) + AI customization becoming the fastest path to market

what_to_watch: Whether AI-built products face trust or quality perception issues as buyers become aware of how they're made
Emergence of 'SaaS factory' operators who systematically launch multiple products per quarter
Consolidation of rapid-prototyping tools (Bolt, Lovable, v0) vs IDE-native agents
